commit.message:
network: Speed up peer connection timings

- PEER_WAIT_SECS: 15s → 5s (initial wait for peers)
- GENESIS_WAIT_SECS: 20s → 10s (genesis block response wait)
- BASE_DISCOVERY_WAIT: 30s → 10s (discovery round backoff base)
- Peer exchange broadcast: 60s → 30s (GetMasternodes interval)
- Health monitoring start: 120s → 30s, interval 120s → 60s
- Peer discovery loop: 120s → 30s (PHASE 3 rediscovery)

Best-case startup to connected drops from ~43s to ~18s.

commit.message:
chore: add Lua-specific Copilot code review instructions (#5)

Add .github/instructions/lua.instructions.md scoped to **/*.lua with
applyTo front matter so GitHub Copilot applies it automatically during
code review. Covers six areas:

- Simplicity and minimal changes: flag over-engineering, prefer early
  returns over deep nesting, change only what the task requires.
- No redundant guards: one check per invariant per execution path,
  trust established nil/validity invariants downstream.
- Execution path coverage: every branch reachable, async coroutines
  must not be silently abandoned on error paths.
- LuaDoc annotations: @param/@return on all public M.* functions,
  @type on class-shaped returns, @async on plenary-wrapped functions,
  types.lua as single source of type truth.
- Idiomatic Neovim Lua: prefer vim.api.* over deprecated vim.fn
  option APIs, use vim.system() for subprocesses, correct ipairs/pairs
  usage, table.concat over .. in loops, strict M module pattern.
- Performance: no shell-outs in loops/events, bulk buffer reads,
  local-variable caching in loops, nvim_chan_send for terminal I/O,
  flag O(n²) patterns, validate before assuming impact.

Also extend .github/copilot-instructions.md with a working guidelines
section covering Lua code change rules, git workflow conventions, and
a revised PR description format: formatted with subtitles, bullet
points for lists, and code samples where applicable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(scripts): add --config-preview and deployment summary to submission scripts (#499)

Ten submission scripts in `scripts/` lacked `--config-preview` and
`section "Deployment Summary"` / `print_kv` calls required by shell
script conventions.

### Changes

- **All 10 scripts** (`submit-azureml-training.sh`,
`submit-azureml-validation.sh`, `submit-azureml-lerobot-training.sh`,
`submit-azureml-lerobot-inference.sh`, `submit-osmo-training.sh`,
`submit-osmo-dataset-training.sh`, `submit-osmo-inference.sh`,
`submit-osmo-lerobot-training.sh`, `submit-osmo-lerobot-inference.sh`,
`run-lerobot-pipeline.sh`) now support:
- `--config-preview` — prints key configuration via `print_kv` and exits
before any side effects (env registration, payload packaging, job
submission)
- `section "Deployment Summary"` with `print_kv` at script end, showing
completed operation context

### Pattern

Follows the established convention from `deploy/002-setup/` scripts:

```bash
# After validation, before side effects
if [[ "$config_preview" == "true" ]]; then
  section "Configuration Preview"
  print_kv "Task" "$task"
  print_kv "Compute" "${compute:-<not set>}"
  # ...
  exit 0
fi

# ... main logic ...

# End of script
section "Deployment Summary"
print_kv "Job Name" "$job_result"
print_kv "Task" "$task"
```

`shellcheck` passes on all modified files with no new warnings.

commit.message:
fix(edxorg): correct parse_archive_path regression and add partition reconciliation

Commit 2c2b9c3a narrowed DATA_ATTRIBUTE_REGEX inclusion to db_table-only
files, breaking course_id extraction for .json (course_structure) and
.xml.tar.gz (course_xml) archives.  Without the regex consuming the
-course- / -course_structure- token the engine backtracked and absorbed
it into course_id, producing invalid partition keys such as
MITx-15.415x-3T2018-course|edge instead of MITx-15.415x-3T2018|edge.

Changes:
- edxorg/lib/edxorg.py: restore DATA_ATTRIBUTE_REGEX for course_structure
  files; add XML_COURSE_SUFFIX_REGEX for course_xml files to strip the
  optional trailing -course suffix from xml.tar.gz filenames (pre-existing
  bug never previously caught).
- edxorg/assets/edxorg_archive.py: guard output_key routing with an
  explicit data_category == 'db_table' check so json files whose
  asset_info now carries table_name='course' are not misrouted to the
  db_table__course output; extract table_name unconditionally to avoid a
  NameError for non-db_table iterations.
- edxorg_tests/test_edxorg_lib.py: 20-test regression suite for
  categorize_archive_element and parse_archive_path covering json, xml,
  sql, and mongo file types; all pass.
- scripts/reconcile_edxorg_partitions.py: one-shot migration script that
  lists bad course_and_source dynamic partition keys, S3-copies affected
  objects to corrected paths, re-emits AssetMaterialization events under
  the canonical partition, and deletes the invalid partition keys.
  Supports --dry-run, --aws-profile, and --s3-bucket flags.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract `SshSession` struct to eliminate SSH arg duplication (#116)

* Initial plan

* refactor(backup): extract SshSession struct to eliminate SSH arg duplication

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(backup): add scp_from and fix scp_to missing ControlMaster flags

SshSession::scp_to was missing ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist
options, preventing SCP from reusing multiplexed SSH connections. Add a
shared scp_args() helper for both scp_to and the new scp_from method,
then delegate scp_from_remote and scp_to_remote to SshSession.

* refactor(backup): delegate remote_ssh_command functions to SshSession

remote_ssh_command and remote_ssh_command_raw now use SshSession::run
instead of manually building SSH args, eliminating more boilerplate.

* style(backup): remove doc comments from SshSession methods

* refactor(backup): DRY up SSH mux options and shell-escape key path in rsync

Extract SSH_MUX_OPTIONS constant and SshSession::mux_args() to eliminate
triplication of ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist across ssh_args,
scp_args, and rsync_e_arg. Also shell-escape the SSH key path in
rsync_e_arg to handle paths with spaces or special characters.

* fix(backup): capture stderr in scp_to/scp_from for better error diagnostics

Replace Stdio::null() with output capture so SCP error messages are
included in the bail message when transfers fail.

* test(backup): add unit tests for SshSession arg builders

Cover ssh_args, scp_args, rsync_e_arg, and mux_args with focused tests
verifying ControlMaster options, port flag casing, key path escaping,
and user@host formatting.

* refactor(backup): rename remote_ssh_command_raw to remote_ssh_command_unchecked

Clarify that this function skips exit-status checking (used by
pg_restore which emits non-fatal warnings), avoiding confusion with
SshSession::run_raw which accepts pre-split args.

---------

commit.message:
feat: Transactional Outbox Implementation (#21) (#22)

* feat: implement Transactional Outbox pattern (ADR-007)

Implements the Transactional Outbox mechanism to guarantee At-Least-Once
message delivery to Kafka, eliminating Dual Write risk.

Changes:
- New platform-infrastructure module with outbox infrastructure
- MessagePublisher port in platform-common
- MessageOutboxEntity (JPA, UUID v7, JSONB payload/headers)
- IntegrationEventListener (@EventListener, same-tx write)
- OutboxRelayService (@Scheduled + ShedLock, batch 100, 1s visibility buffer)
- KafkaMessagePublisher (KafkaTemplate, x-tenant-id/x-message-type/x-correlation-id headers)
- OutboxCleanupService (7-day retention for SENT records)
- Exponential backoff with max 5 retries, then FAILED status
- Flyway migration V6 for message_outbox + shedlock tables
- Kafka config in application.yml (outbox.enabled=true)

Tests:
- OutboxIntegrationTest (Testcontainers: PostgreSQL + Kafka)
  - Verifies PENDING records created in same transaction
  - Verifies relay sends to Kafka and marks SENT
  - Verifies Kafka headers (x-tenant-id, x-message-type, x-correlation-id)
- OutboxArchitectureTest (ArchUnit)
  - All adapter implementations are package-private
  - MessagePublisher is an interface
  - Domain does not depend on outbox infrastructure

Closes #21

* fix(outbox): address review feedback — correctness, portability, configurability, coverage (#23)

- Add @ConditionalOnProperty to KafkaMessagePublisher (was missing)

- Add OutboxCleanupServiceTest with 2 unit tests

# Description

New Copilot agent skill for maintaining contributor documentation
(`docs/contributing/`, `docs/architecture/`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`). The
skill provides structured workflows for creating, rewriting, reviewing,
and gap-analyzing contributor docs, plus a code review doc impact
assessment mode.

### Added
- `.github/skills/contributing-docs-updater/SKILL.md` — skill definition
with five modes: rewrite, create, gap analysis, doc review, and code
review impact assessment. Uses dynamic directory discovery instead of
hardcoded paths.
-
`.github/skills/contributing-docs-updater/references/writing-guidelines.md`
— writing standards reference covering two content types (Guide and
Reference), formatting, tone, cross-reference rules, and content
accuracy checks.

skills index.
- `.github/instructions/code-review.instructions.md` — added Step 4
(Assess Contributor Documentation Impact) to the code review checklist,
linking to the new skill.

or other maintenance task and doesn't change the functionality of Radius

commit.message:
Harden non-coding agent guardrails with command allowlist and guard workflow (#178)

* Harden non-coding agent guardrails with command allowlist and guard workflow

Problem: The project agent violated its guardrails on 2026-03-09 by editing
code via the execute tool, pushing commits, and closing issues as fixed when
code was never merged to main (PR #174, issues #168/#175).

Root cause: The denylist approach (NEVER run git commit) was insufficient.
The agent rationalized around prohibitions when it found bugs to fix.

Three-layer defense:

1. Command ALLOWLIST (project.agent.md, product.agent.md):
   - Exhaustive list of permitted commands (gh issue, curl, cat/grep/jq)
   - Everything else FORBIDDEN (git add/commit/push, sed, gh pr create)
   - CHECKPOINT reminders at every phase transition
   - Identity anchoring: 'You are an ANALYST, not an engineer'
   - Issue-close verification: must confirm fix exists in main branch

2. Guard workflow (guard-agent-prs.yml):
   - Triggers on pull_request opened/reopened
   - Auto-closes PRs from non-coding agent branch patterns
   - Hard platform-level boundary that can't be bypassed by prompts

3. Documentation updates:
   - PROJECT-AGENT.md: describes allowlist and guard workflow
   - ARCHITECTURE.md: updated agent isolation section

commit.message:
Unify TUI rendering: ScreenMap replaces WrapMap, single render path

WrapMap renamed to ScreenMap — handles both word-wrap and no-wrap modes.
In no-wrap mode, content_width is usize::MAX so nothing wraps. Query
result lines (LineSource::QueryResult) are never wrapped.

The two TUI render paths (draw_editor_content_nowrap and
draw_editor_content_wrapped) are merged into one unified function
that always uses ScreenMap + ScreenScroll. This fixes:

- R1: Core scan-to-fill stops producing lines when screen is full,
  preventing query result expansion from pushing buffer lines off-screen.
- R2: Cursor Y computed by scanning visible_lines for the buffer line,
  then mapping through ScreenMap. The old arithmetic
  (cursor.line - scroll_offset) that broke with injected query lines
  is deleted.

The buggy no-wrap cursor positioning in draw_pane (which assumed 1:1
buffer-line-to-screen-row mapping) is removed. ScreenMap handles cursor
placement for all modes.

ScreenMap.find_buffer_line() helper scans visible_lines for a given
buffer line index — used for cursor entry lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: repair welcome and copilot-review CI workflows (#118)

welcome.yml: actions/first-interaction v1.3.0 requires underscore input
names (repo_token, issue_message, pr_message) not kebab-case. The
kebab-case variants were silently ignored, causing 'Input required and
not supplied: issue_message' error on every new contributor PR/issue.

copilot-review.yml: Switch from pull_request to pull_request_target so
the workflow gets write permissions on fork PRs. Add || echo fallback
so the job doesn't fail with HTTP 403 when Copilot review can't be
requested (graceful degradation instead of hard failure).

commit.message:
Add complete backend Python codebase for MAMA CHOL VPN

- config/settings.py: Pydantic-settings with all environment variables
- models/database.py: SQLAlchemy ORM models (User, Subscription, VPNConfig, Payment, Server, SupportTicket, PromoCode)
- utils/encryption.py: JWT tokens, bcrypt hashing, HMAC signing, secure token generation
- utils/helpers.py: UUID, QR code, byte formatting, plan limits, input sanitization
- services/currency_exchange.py: Live rates with Redis caching, fixed prices for BDT/USD/CNY/INR
- services/email_service.py: Gmail SMTP with Jinja2 HTML templates
- services/vpn_manager.py: X-UI/Xray integration for VLESS/VMess/Trojan/Shadowsocks
- services/ai_chatbot.py: Ollama AI chatbot with EN/BN/ZH/HI/AR support
- api/main.py: FastAPI app with CORS, middleware, routers, health/chat/sub endpoints
- api/auth.py: Register, login, refresh, logout, password reset
- api/user.py: Profile, dashboard, VPN config management
- api/vpn.py: Server listing, usage stats, connect endpoint
- api/payment.py: bKash/Nagad/Stripe/Crypto payments, webhook, manual verification
- api/admin.py: Dashboard metrics, user/server management, payment approval, analytics
